title: HOKUM
review: Movie was an absolute banger. Adam Scott killed this one. I went into the movie knowing nothing about it except that one super creepy poster and what I got was something completely different, something that wasn't so much a horror movie as it was a character movie. The writing around Bauman (Scott) is so phenomenal that the scariness and unsettling feelings the picture brings gets washed away every time the screen is not dark. When it's dark though it feels claustrophobic - set design really plays a huge part in the tension you feel when watching. I think some of the witch stuff could have been a bit scarier; it's just an ugly old woman. The actual idea of the hotel having a lot unknown  monsters behind its walls is more scary then the witch. I think at some parts I was like ok what is happening but everything kind of pointed towards the main character and his backstory in a great way, to the point where I could ignore the sudden switches in movie style. When I say that I mean some parts looked like cheap horror and the other parts really meshed together well, the parts where we see other characters. All in all great film

title: Michael
review:
Mmmmmm honestly not the best movie but not boring at all. I think the biggest flaw of this movie was that the lead actor wasn't Michael Jackson. I didn't come from that era of music but all I know about the king of pop is that he made girls faint left and right and would move like he was a spring, almost alien/machine like. 

I mean the actor did a great job. I feel like I see his face now when I think of Michael Jackson, but that might be because I didn't really grow up listening to MJ.

The highlight of the movie was definitely Michael as a kid singing his heart out in the studio. That kid really killed it, he sounded just like Michael in the recordings. His movement and expressions really seemed authentic.

The movie had me looking up Michael's lore after. I didn't know that he had burned his head or had exotic pets - I wish they got into some of the weirder stuff that happened later in his career. I really enjoyed the acting of Joseph Jackson - he really did a good job portraying a cold father who simultaneously loves his family and is blinded by greed.

I think the coolest part about this movie is that someone from my high school was playing Tito Jackson. He actually had a couple of good lines in the movie too. Ryan Hill - good job ur making Torrance proud.
